ideas.headline: Forced-buy surface: Greek AI Act competent-authority timeline (Aug 2025 designation, Dec 2026 transposition) plus newly created Special Secretariat for AI inside the renamed Ministry of Digital Governance and AI = the buyer literally cannot ship without a Greek-language eval substrate. Precedents (AISI/AI Security Institute, AI Verify Moonshot, DINUM Albert eval) all verified operational in 2025-2
blockers: Hiring a credible AI-policy lawyer with Greek-EU AI Act fluency is the bottleneck — ILSP/Athena RC ML researchers are easier to recruit than this profile
Risk of being mistaken for a notified body; must position as pre-conformity assistance, not the legally binding assessor
Open-sourcing the Greek benchmark (the buyerHook commitment) is a tax on capital efficiency — must scope the open-source surface tightly
ideas.next30days: Identify the named lead of the Special Secretariat for AI and Data Governance inside the renamed Ministry of Digital Governance and AI; request a 30-minute scoping meeting · Draft a 4-page memo mapping AI Act Annex IV requirements to a Greek-specific eval rubric; share with one friendly ministry CDO (Migration or AADE) for reaction

ideas.headline: PM-level tailwind and operational precedent are fully fresh: Bürokratt is actively rolling out LLM/RAG across Estonian agencies through 2025, TAMM 3.0 + AutoGov is now the global benchmark (Oct 2025), and the Ministry was renamed to include AI in July 2025. The mitos process-graph is a real public asset no other vendor is traversing. Single significant blocker is Netcompany's incumbency claim on t
blockers: Netcompany (ex-Intrasoft) will argue extension of gov.gr is theirs by incumbency — must contract for the agent runtime + Greek NLU layer, not the portal
EU AI Act art. 6 high-risk classification for essential public services — needs ISO 42001-style ops, human-in-loop on every submission, transparency notice
Per-ministry API rate limits and back-office endpoint inconsistency — orchestration depends on ministries cooperating, which is a political handshake not a technical one
TaxisNet auth + Wallet.gov.gr credential pre-fetch requires explicit ΓΓΠΣΨΔ data-protection sign-off (DPIA)
ideas.next30days: Memo-ready pitch to the gov.gr product owner + General Secretary for Information Systems & Digital Governance; lead with the 'Greek-language process-graph traversal' framing, not 'gov.gr agent' · Pick 3 high-traffic cross-ministerial journeys to demo end-to-end (vehicle transfer, business start-up, change-of-address) — cite KEP foot-traffic baseline by periphery

ideas.headline: Strongest precedent freshness in the batch: UK Redbox at 2,000+ civil servants Feb 2025 and growing 150/week; Pair Singapore at 60,000+ users with 46% admin-time savings; GovTech Singapore published an Agentic AI Primer April 2025. France Services Albert pilot will NOT be generalized as-is per Jan 2026 DINUM statement — this is a useful lesson, not a kill (the back-office productivity story is sti
blockers: M365 Copilot ministry-wide rollout via Azure Athens landed-zone is the dominant procurement narrative; we must be a plug-in, not a competitor

Albert at France Services will not be generalized in current form (DINUM, Jan 2026) — we need to internalize why and pre-empt the same failure pattern (likely: insufficient task fit at front-line + cost)
KEP/civil-servant change-management is harder than the tech; need an ex-civil-servant operations lead, not just an engineer
Διαύγεια publication metadata pre-fill must clear legal validation — admin acts have specific publishability rules
ideas.next30days: Read the DINUM Albert-at-France-Services post-mortem in detail; write a 1-page 'what we do differently' brief · Validate the M365 Copilot plug-in story with a Microsoft Greece account exec — confirm Azure Athens landed-zone timeline and plug-in extensibility model

ideas.headline: 10DS / Evidence House precedent verified fresh (community of 500+ engineers/analysts across UK government, 2025). Singapore PMO + Pair confirmed. Estonia X-Road remains the substrate reference. The kill risk is NOT precedent — it is the political-third-rail axis: every government change re-opens the contract. Επικρατείας anchor is the mitigation but it is not yet a permanent function. Pilot procur
blockers: Pilot procurability at 2: each ministry endpoint requires a separate political handshake; not designable in 6 months
Political-appointee dependency — contract durability is tied to PM tenure unless we land inside Επικρατείας as a permanent function
GDPR + Hellenic DPA scrutiny on cross-ministerial joining is structural, not a one-time DPIA — we need a permanent privacy lawyer in the founding team
Engineer security clearance pathway in Greece is not well-documented for non-state-owned vendors; sovereign-hosting on GRNet is the only credible answer
Founder-market-fit at 3: requires an ex-civil-servant with Επικρατείας access who is recruitable but not abundant
ideas.next30days: Scope a non-political pilot use case: parliamentary-questions desk officers (a permanent function across governments) rather than PM-office strategy work · Open conversation with GRNet leadership about the sovereign-hosting story before approaching Επικρατείας — this neutralises the trust objection in advance

ideas.headline: Greece fully implemented Law 4994/2022 transposing the EAA, in force June 28 2025. EETT enforces digital-service compliance with fines €10k-€100k. The European Commission separately called on Greece to fully transpose (March 2025), signalling political pressure. Buyer is identifiable (ΓΓΠΣΨΔ accessibility lead + EETT) but the political tailwind is regulator-driven rather than PM-directive, and Deq
blockers: Two buyers (ΓΓΠΣΨΔ accessibility lead + EETT) with no clear single decision-maker — slower than a single-champion sale
Global scanner incumbents (Deque, Siteimprove, Level Access) have public-sector references and will pitch direct
Greek-plain-language rewriting from καθαρευουσιάνικη legalese is the moat — but it is also the hardest part to build and demo convincingly without a content-licensing arrangement with ΓΓΠΣΨΔ
ESAmEA endorsement is necessary for political weight but adds a third stakeholder
Studio fit at 3: this is a compliance-platform play, less agentic than the other four; risk of becoming a forms-and-PDFs SaaS
ideas.next30days: Validate that EETT is genuinely the enforcement authority for gov.gr properties (vs only private-sector digital services) — read Law 4994/2022 implementing decree carefully · Build a 30-second demo: take one bureaucratic gov.gr form, run Greek plain-language rewrite, show before/after WCAG 2.2 AA compliance — this must be impressive or kill the idea
